2604.23494.title: Do Transaction-Level and Actor-Level AML Queues Agree? An Empirical Evaluation of Granularity Effects on the Elliptic++ Graph
2604.23494.paper_summary: Graph-based anti-money laundering (AML) systems on blockchain networks can score suspicious activity at two granularity levels -- transactions or actor addresses -- yet compliance action is conducted per actor. This paper contributes an evaluation methodology for measuring how scoring granularity affects investigation queue composition under fixed review budgets. We formalize the evaluation through a projection framework mapping transaction-level scores to the actor-level action unit via four aggregation operators, and introduce budgeted investigation metrics -- yield@budget, burden decomposition, and case fragmentation. Using the public Elliptic++ Bitcoin dataset (203,769 transactions; 822,942 address occurrences), we train independent random forest classifiers at each level under a causal temporal protocol and compare review queues through Jaccard overlap, burden decomposition, and feature-matching ablations. At one-percent budget, temporal evaluation yields mean Jaccard of 0.374 (SD 0.171); static pooled evaluation yields 0.087 (95% CI [0.079, 0.094]). An enriched address model receiving all 237 features produces even lower overlap (Jaccard=0.051), with 4.3% illicit per 100 reviews versus 30.2% for the transaction-projected queue. Address-level detection value is temporally concentrated: two timesteps exceed 91% illicit per 100 reviews while the static burden is only 3.4%. A fixed hybrid policy underperforms the best single-level queue by 5.05pp (CI [-10.2pp, -0.9pp]). These findings establish that scoring granularity is a consequential design variable for AML investigation systems -- same data, same budget, different queues, different addresses investigated.
2604.23494.paper_summary_zh: 基於圖形的反洗錢 (AML) 系統在區塊鏈網絡上可以在兩個粒度層級上評分可疑活動——交易或行為者地址——但合規行動是根據行為者進行的。本文貢獻了一種評估方法，用於測量評分粒度如何影響在固定審查預算下的調查隊列組成。我們通過一個投影框架形式化評估，將交易層級的分數映射到行為者層級的行動單位，並通過四個聚合運算子引入預算調查指標——yield@budget、負擔分解和案件碎片化。使用公共的 Elliptic++ 比特幣數據集（203,769 笔交易；822,942 地址出現），我們在因果時間協議下對每個層級訓練獨立的隨機森林分類器，並通過 Jaccard 重疊、負擔分解和特徵匹配消融比較審查隊列。在一百分之一的預算下，時間評估的平均 Jaccard 為 0.374（標準差 0.171）；靜態池評估為 0.087（95% 置信區間 [0.079, 0.094]）。接收所有 237 個特徵的增強地址模型產生了更低的重疊（Jaccard=0.051），每 100 次審查中有 4.3% 的非法活動，與交易投影隊列的 30.2% 相比。地址層級的檢測價值在時間上是集中性的：兩個時間步驟每 100 次審查中超過 91% 的非法活動，而靜態負擔僅為 3.4%。固定混合政策的表現比最佳單層隊列低 5.05 個百分點（置信區間 [-10.2pp, -0.9pp]）。這些發現確立了評分粒度是反洗錢調查系統的一個重要設計變量——相同數據、相同預算、不同隊列、不同地址被調查。
2604.23494.author: Ankur Malik et.al.
